edges.label: triggered_retaliation
edges.why: Reuters reports Hormuz closure threat was triggered by U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

edges.label: caused
edges.why: Closure threat and attacks led vessels to halt/anchor and insurers to withdraw cover, disrupting flows.

edges.label: amplified_costs
edges.why: Reuters notes freight rates and war-risk pricing spiked as traffic halted and attacks occurred.

edges.label: drives
edges.why: Hormuz is a major chokepoint for oil/LNG; closure risk increases crude supply risk premium.

edges.label: reinforces
edges.why: Broader energy supply shock (gas/LNG) tends to support oil-linked risk premia and cross-commodity substitution.

edges.label: feeds
edges.why: Higher delivered energy/freight costs raise inflation risk and can widen credit spreads for transport/industrial sectors.

edges.label: drives
edges.why: Higher import bills and supply disruptions worsen trade balances for energy importers, pressuring EM FX.

edges.label: drives
edges.why: A widening multi-front conflict raises tail risk, typically pushing investors into safe assets.
